name: hunch-vscode
description: Your repo's engineering memory, in the editor: see WHY code is the way it is (decisions, invariants, bug history), and capture what you decide — one command each.
author: Dave Sheffer <dave.sheffer1@gmail.com>

commands.title: Why is this symbol the way it is?

hunch.statusBar.enabled.description: Show how many invariants guard the active file in the status bar.

hunch.hover.enabled.description: Show Hunch bug history and fragility when hovering a symbol.

hunch.cliPath.description: Command used to invoke the Hunch CLI for capture (set an absolute path if `hunch` is not on PATH).

languageModelTools.name: hunch-vscode_why
languageModelTools.userDescription: Why a file or symbol is the way it is — decisions, invariants, bug history, and blast radius from the repo's Hunch engineering memory.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the recorded engineering memory for a file path or symbol in this repository: invariants that MUST NOT break (with severity and rationale), near-invariants reached through the dependency blast radius, the decisions that shaped the code (including rejected alternatives), bug history with root causes, and direct dependents. Call BEFORE modifying or reviewing a file to avoid breaking intentional design. Input: a workspace-relative file path (e.g. src/auth/session.ts) or a symbol name.

target.description: Workspace-relative file path or symbol name.

languageModelTools.name: hunch-vscode_context
languageModelTools.userDescription: The pre-edit brief for a file: everything Hunch knows that an edit could break, plus its fragile symbols.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Assembles the minimal relevant engineering-memory context before working on a file: invariants in scope, near-invariants, shaping decisions, bug history, blast radius, and the file's fragile symbols (bug count / churn / fan-in signal). Call FIRST when starting non-trivial work on a file in a repo with a .hunch/ directory. Input: a workspace-relative file path.

languageModelTools.name: hunch-vscode_query
languageModelTools.userDescription: Free-text search across the repo's recorded decisions, invariants, bugs, and components.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Free-text search over every record in the repository's Hunch engineering-memory graph (decisions, invariants/constraints, bugs, components). Use to answer 'was this decided before?', 'has this failed before?', or to find the record governing a topic. Returns ranked matches with ids and related files. Input: a search query.

query.description: What to search the graph for.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Hunch
walkthroughs.description: See why code is the way it is; capture what you decide.

steps.title: Why is this the way it is?
steps.description: One command (and hover, and the status bar) answers it: invariants, decisions, bug history, blast radius.

steps.title: Capture what you decide
steps.description: Hunch: Capture… records a decision, invariant, or bug into the repo's memory.
